requirements.statement: Constrain each agent's file edits to its assigned workspace.
Agents may propose only typed results and privileged-action requests; the orchestrator exclusively owns tracker, repository-hosting, control-plane, notification, and merge mutations.
End successful work at the workflow-defined handoff state rather than assuming `Done`.
Ground agent progress in runnable verification, small diffs, and early plan checks rather than ceremonial procedure.
Use budgets as hard control inputs, persist spend by Attempt, issue, SystemJob, and rolling fleet window, and let remaining budget affect routing.
Make guard and merge decisions from orchestrator-owned durable records; tracker comments are projections and never decision inputs.
Scale process deterministically by change class so trivial, standard, and high-risk work take proportionate routes.
Check readiness and plan quality before implementation, correctness before merge, and feed escaped defects back into routing.
Earn merge autonomy per change class from measured outcomes, require operator grant, and revoke it automatically after an escaped defect.
Record failures, findings, and confusions as lessons and periodically synthesize them into prompt/rule proposals under hard saturation caps.
Require every lane, field, rule, and configuration key to justify its cost; prefer attributes, deletion, and one strong default.
The Workflow Loader reads and validates `WORKFLOW.md` and commits immutable configuration snapshots.
The Orchestrator owns polling, dispatch, claims, retries, reconciliation, budgets, and merge queue, and is the sole durable control-plane writer.
The single-writer local SQLite store durably holds the complete named control-plane record catalog.
The Workspace Manager owns issue/SystemJob directories, repository population, hooks, quarantine, and cleanup under a configured root.

The Agent Runner launches in-workspace processes, streams bounded events, enforces limits, accepts at most one role-valid result, fails missing/duplicate results, and terminates the full process tree.
The Review Coordinator runs deterministic checks, dispatches required reviewers/specialists, merges findings without voting, and persists review records.
The Merge Queue serializes by repository and revalidates every merge against the current base.
Tracker and repository-hosting adapters normalize reads and perform only authorized, revision-pinned mutations.
The Learning Synthesizer turns lessons into proposed prompt/rule changes through the same review pipeline as code.
The Notifier invokes the configured notification hook for events requiring human attention.
The authenticated Operator Control API and required browser UI operate over the same durable state used by orchestration.
Structured logs, append-only events, durable history, and stage timing are visible through the required UI.
Normalize every Issue field and invariant: identity, criteria, lane, lowercased labels, nullable integer priority, blocker states, assignee, repository, URL, and timestamps.
Enforce the Attempt role, work-ref, routing, compute, usage, lifecycle-status, terminal-record, and end-time schema and enums.
Store input, output, and total token fields on every durable Attempt.
Store `cost_usd` when the pinned adapter has a price table; otherwise store null and enforce token budgets.

Accept exactly one typed ImplementationOutcome with the listed status, evidence, action, confusion, and handoff semantics; close missing results as ExecutionFailure and keep tracker mutation orchestrator-owned.
Require workspace-verification evidence on a `completed` ImplementationOutcome.
Require a structured question on a `needs_input` ImplementationOutcome.

Enforce the Claim schema, ownership states, four modes, non-expiring reservations, transaction-before-launch, cross-role retention, atomic Attempt closure/next mode, terminal release, and unique work-ref ownership.
Renew each Running lease before expiry; reconcile rather than blindly resume an expired lease.
Rebuild Ready work, retry timers, and reconciliation cursors from durable claim fields after restart.

Store immutable configuration snapshots with hashes, override provenance, redacted secret references, source/version metadata, ack/restart state, adapter versions, and exact Attempt/SystemJob pinning; apply hot/attempt/restart boundaries as specified.

Enforce ReviewRecord and ordinary ReviewSet schemas, immutable target identity, complete required reviewers, current verification/guards, approval prerequisites, no unresolved blockers, and invalidation on revision or patch change.
A carried ReviewSet must link an approved prior ReviewSet with identical patch identity and the complete named reviewer records.
A carried ReviewSet's Verification Record and all non-carry-forward Guard Decisions must target its new head/base; no other cross-revision record is valid.
Persist the complete GuardDecision, append-only Lesson, synthesized Rule, and LiveSession schemas and enums, including evidence/routing citations and process/protocol ownership facts.
Persist RetryEntry, ParkedWork, OperatorQuestion, and AgentApprovalRequest schemas and their first-authorized-answer/no-credential invariants.
A blocker predicate must name a fact the orchestrator can re-check; otherwise only an authorized operator may resolve it.
Keep StageTransitions append-only with the listed fields/enums, one open transition, restricted nullability, atomic close/open, receipt/tracker/estimate timestamps, and reconstructable duration.
When a StageTransition names an Attempt, require that Attempt to share its `work_ref`.

Enforce complete MutationAuthorization, SideEffectIntent, and SideEffectReceipt schemas, scope/target/revision validity, statuses, exact envelope binding, and unresolved-intent idempotency reconciliation.
Commit an authorized SideEffectIntent before calling an external adapter.
Commit a SideEffectReceipt after an external adapter call.
Pass the persisted authorization to every mutating external adapter and reject missing, expired, mismatched, or stale envelopes.
Enforce OperatorAction audit/idempotency, append-only RepositoryLink history, and durable versioned Plan schemas; write `PLAN.md` only as a projection and never recover decisions from Markdown.
Enforce each PlanReview, Review, Adjudication, Synthesis, and ExecutionFailure schema/conditional field plus SystemJob schema/status/aggregate/reuse contracts.
Do not accept one Attempt role's terminal-result schema for another role.
Enforce ServiceRun, LogRecord, UsageSample, BudgetLedger/Reservation/Adjustment, and VerificationRecord schemas, enums, retention/aggregation, and orchestrator-authored verification authority.
Log commands, tool actions, verification, file actions, reviews, and orchestrator decisions with enough structure for stage-oriented UI history.
Do not store secrets, full prompts, hidden reasoning, or unbounded raw payloads.

Implement provisional and authoritative `trivial`/`standard`/`high_risk` classification, conservative unknown handling, configured path/size/risk facts, monotonic reclassification, class-specific plan/review/compute/autonomy routes, and sampled audits.
Base classification only on observable facts, never agent self-assessment.
Before continuing after an upward class change, apply all newly required plan, compute, specialist, and merge restrictions.

Enforce the six lane definitions, valid transitions, Human origins/reasons, reconciliation-first poll ordering, sorted eligibility/preflight, removal behavior, baseline/external lane timing, and estimated-boundary display.
Support a webhook or event-stream trigger for an immediate poll tick.

Execute the complete implementation flow: transactional class/snapshot/budget/claim/lane, workspace/hooks, typed Plan and class gates, bounded high-risk review/revisions/questions, bounded sessions, reclassification, independent verification, authorized publication, and Review transition.
Continuation guidance must include current plan state, last verification, unmet criteria, remaining turn/token budget, and the Plan-revision instruction.
Do not resend the original prompt in continuation guidance.

Atomically map every role outcome/failure to its bounded issue/SystemJob claim, Plan/ReviewSet, lane/stage, parking/retry/publication route; receipt-gate all issue transitions and synthesize no tracker transition for SystemJobs.

Use sanitized issue/SystemJob paths, one claimed mutable workspace, trusted base-SHA checkout provenance, no agent publication, startup quarantine, cross-attempt persistence, and post-history terminal cleanup.
Launch the agent subprocess with `cwd` equal to the assigned workspace.
Resolve the workspace, including symlinks, beneath `workspace.root`; a string-prefix check is insufficient.
Enforce an OS-level write boundary before any agent or hook, limiting writes to the resolved workspace.
Place worker HOME, temporary files, caches, and tool state inside the assigned workspace.
Resolve each write target at operation time and deny external absolute paths, traversal, mount escapes, and escaping symlinks.
Put the agent in a process group; on every stop condition terminate all descendants and verify exit before releasing the lease.

Implement the required verification loop: mandatory command or acknowledged `none`, prompt guidance, independent pinned-revision scrubbed execution, durable VerificationRecord, failure routing, and merge-time required checks.
Require `workspace.verify_command`; permit `none` only with a reason and force supervised merges.
Instruct agents to run the verification command early and often.
Independently run the pinned verification command against the proposed revision in a scrubbed environment and commit a Verification Record.
Run the four hooks with documented fatality, non-login shell, workspace cwd, scrubbed environment, and timeout.
Enforce attempt token/USD, issue USD, rolling fleet USD, and unpriced issue/fleet token scopes with hard-stop, park/pause/notification, delayed-overrun, and automatic-window-resume behavior.
Account from absolute token events with replay-safe deltas, pinned price tables, durable samples/totals, last-known final usage, and issue/role/class/profile/SystemJob/fleet queries.
Convert provider delta usage to absolute session totals inside the adapter.
Do not use generic `usage` maps on non-token events for accounting.
Answer per-issue cost from the durable store with one query.

Reserve all applicable ledgers before dispatch/fan-out using configured/history estimates; atomically top up or stop before another turn; settle/release; record and block overruns; route budget denial and never raise autonomy/lower risk floors.
Do not partially start a fan-out or dispatch whose all-scope reservation does not fit.
Show limit, reserved, consumed, overrun, and remaining budget separately in the UI.
Use logical profiles with pinned per-Attempt model/effort/reasons, documented role/class defaults, deterministic risk floors/predicates/built-ins, bounded fresh escalation, and budget-aware routing.
Workflow policy must not embed provider model slugs; it names logical profiles only.
Heuristic or model classification may raise but never lower a deterministic risk floor.
Configured risk-floor rules must not lower built-in class and role defaults.

Run deterministic prechecks; required integrative/specialist review on one immutable SHA; union/deduplicate without voting; narrow adjudication; atomic complete ReviewSet; fail-closed PR/check/thread/human-approval gate; and durable review economics.
An adjudicator must cite evidence for contrary blocking-finding resolutions.
An adjudicator must not erase an uncontested blocking finding.
Every required check must target the current head and have an accepted terminal conclusion.
Require an open, non-draft, mergeable pull request.

Enforce deterministic branch/repair naming, one open PR, durable projections, per-repository serialization, current-head/base/review/autonomy eligibility, base updates, landing/post-merge checks, rework, and linked repair SystemJobs.
On identical patch identity after a base update, write an allowing carry-forward Guard Decision and a new current-head ReviewSet linked to the prior set.

Enforce per-class supervised/auto autonomy, permanent high-risk supervision, evidence-backed operator promotion, automatic escaped-defect demotion/notification, supervised synthesis/verify-none, and authorized current-head approval.

Render/consume structured questions; reconcile/remind parked work; send idempotent authorized notifications for every named event; and expose all versioned/capability/idempotency-audited operator controls.
Configure stable operator records with auth subject, capabilities, and optional tracker login; atomically accept only the first authorized question answer.
Use authenticated UI/API controls for budget, autonomy, config acknowledgment/overrides, merge-queue retry/skip, synthesis, retention, and refresh; audit capability, version, and idempotency.
Keep distinct required capabilities; do not collapse all mutations into read access.

Capture every listed lesson automatically; trigger budgeted deep synthesis by interval/operator; publish typed cited changes/no-change/question; run supervised review/merge; enforce active-job and rule/prompt/decay caps.
Propose removal of rules uncited for `learning.rule_decay_issues` completed issues at the next synthesis.

Classify and route infrastructure/agent/config/auth/policy/task failures with the specified retry accounting, backoff/jitter/retry-after, scoped pause/lessons/notifications, stall kill, durable restart reconciliation, intent idempotency, and persistence safety stop.
Stop existing workers when lease renewal, usage accounting, or terminal-result persistence is unsafe.

Persist/export structured logs and typed EventRecords with required fields, durable cost queries/history, indefinite default retention, reproducible sampled audits, escaped-defect classification, and per-class/route quality metrics driving autonomy/synthesis.
Make startup, validation, dispatch, agent, command, tool, verification, review, merge, and failure logs visible in the UI.
EventRecords must not contain prompts, diffs, or secrets.
Ship a working browser UI.
The UI must use the Control API, not SQLite, tracker comments, or humanized log strings as decision state.
Show all operations-dashboard counts, running/retry/parked/SystemJob/queue state, all budget facets and totals, failures, notifications, and durable issue/PR links.
Show complete issue/run history, stages, attempts, referenced SystemJobs, evidence, side effects, snapshots, usage, logs/actions, prior runs, and restarts.
Support paginated, filterable logs with live updates after durable write.
Show all settings metadata and provide every required settings/control mutation and audit surface.
Identify stale data and API errors visibly.
Preserve a failed mutation's submitted value and structured error.
Do not show an uncommitted optimistic value as effective configuration.
Authenticated API must provide state, budget, quality, notification, and repository-queue reads.
Authenticated API must paginate every listed durable resource read.
Authenticated API must expose effective configuration and source metadata.
Authenticated API must create, update, and clear overrides and acknowledge privileged candidates.
Authenticated API must answer questions, decide agent approvals, change/reset budgets and autonomy, retry/skip queues, dispatch synthesis, update retention, and refresh.
Require `operator.read` on every read and the matching Section 11.4 capability on mutations.
Authenticate and capability-check mutations; require expected version, scoped idempotency, same-origin/CSRF, TLS remotely, and loopback default.
Mark auth cookies Secure under TLS, HttpOnly, and SameSite Lax or stricter.
Escape untrusted UI data, sanitize rich text, reject unsafe URLs, and enforce a CSP without inline scripts.
Return the specified structured error envelope and use `401`, `403`, and `422` for unauthenticated, unauthorized, and invalid requests.
Never return secrets from the Control API.
Persist an OperatorAction with actor, capability, target version, reason, and result for every accepted or rejected authenticated mutation.
Persist a rate-limited security LogRecord for unauthenticated attempts without storing supplied credentials.
Default retention to indefinite and keep every named completed-history record queryable and visible after completion and restart; audit any authorized finite-policy change and deletion.
Retention deletion must not remove active work, unresolved side effects, held records, or aggregates needed for current budgets/autonomy.
Store and show a durable deletion tombstone with scope, range, actor, policy version, deletion time, and retained aggregates.
Do not present deleted history as if no activity occurred.
Prevent agents from recovering host credentials through environment, HOME, sockets, or mounts.
Use `$VAR` indirection for configuration secrets; reject literal tokens in `WORKFLOW.md`.
Give agents and hooks only an allowlisted minimal environment and isolated credential-free HOME; resolve secrets only at adapter boundaries and filter credentials before model or durable context.

Hold every authority/execution/credential candidate behind an exact-hash, versioned OperatorAction acknowledgment; keep last known good values active, reject invalid acknowledgment, and apply only at the declared reload boundary.

Enter pre-auth bootstrap only on a post-migration pristine store; bind loopback, disable orchestration and external mutation, apply the narrow operator-mapping validation exemption, and require a structurally valid auth adapter.
Treat an operator-empty non-pristine store as corruption: reconcile recorded processes and unresolved intents, record `operator_store_missing_nonpristine`, then exit for known-good restoration.
On an operator-empty non-pristine store, reconcile owned processes/intents without issuing a new mutation or dispatch.
Do not create a replacement administrator in an operator-empty non-pristine store.
Do not expose unauthenticated remote setup or reuse the bootstrap credential.

Require a trusted single-use credential and local full-hash confirmation; atomically create the first administrator, auth mapping, acknowledged snapshot, and OperatorAction; roll back on failure and enable normal service only after full validation.
Document each deployment's agent approval and sandbox posture.
Do not let approval requests or input-required signals stall indefinitely; satisfy, surface, or fail by policy.
Enforce the Section 6.1 write boundary for agents and hooks in every deployment.

Persist surfaced agent approvals, park without consuming a slot where supported, expose them through UI/API, authorize only `agent.approve`, audit decision scope/expiry, resume origin stage, and type timeout/unsupported failures.
Map a verified credential to an immutable `auth_subject` matching one effective operator record.
Document session lifetime, logout/revocation, credential storage, and subject mapping.

Store local credentials only as salted verifier records; fully validate OIDC; trust identity headers only on authenticated proxy paths; and exclude credentials/session tokens from URLs, logs, events, snapshots, and API responses.
Re-evaluate active sessions after operator/capability changes and revoke removed access before the next request.
If guided setup is supplied, implement the ordered tracker/credential/project/schema/workflow/validation flow, restrict outputs, confirm each tracker mutation, and hand off to pristine bootstrap.
Ship a guided pre-start command-line setup; if shipped, follow the complete setup contract.
A provided setup tool must not create durable-store records.
A provided setup tool must not bind a network listener.
A provided setup tool must not store state outside its allowed workflow/environment/tracker outputs.
Setup must not write a literal credential into `WORKFLOW.md`.
Setup must not mutate tracker schema without per-change operator confirmation.
Setup must be idempotent and resume by detecting completed steps and prompting only for gaps.
Tracker adapter must implement candidate/state/startup reads, lane update, comment upsert, incremental comments, and cursor pagination.
Fail an incomplete tracker page instead of treating partial data as complete.
Classify tracker adapter errors under Section 13; skip a tick on candidate-fetch failure while leaving existing workers running on state-refresh failure.
Tracker mutations must reject missing or mismatched authorization and stale tracker revisions.
Return provider request identifiers and resulting tracker revisions for SideEffectReceipts.

The GitHub reference profile uses Issues and Projects v2 with Status lanes, Priority ordering, native blockers, non-config-token authentication, configured repository scope, and Section 3.1 normalization.
Do not invent acceptance criteria from prose; only normalize structured fields or configured checklist headings.
The running service must not invoke the optional pre-store project-schema repair operation.
An advertised project-schema extension must test idempotent reruns and partial-schema repair.
Repository-hosting adapter must publish branches, manage PRs, fetch complete snapshots, update branches, merge, fetch post-merge status, and create repair PRs.
Return the complete normalized PullRequestSnapshot schema: identity/state, head/base, mergeability, check-source and checks, review decision and reviews, unresolved threads, and post-merge checks.
Return complete review, check, and thread data or fail the snapshot.
Partial repository pagination must not appear empty or successful.
Repository mutations must reject missing/mismatched authorization or stale revisions.
Expose provider request identifiers and result revisions for receipts.
Do not expose repository-hosting credentials to agent or reviewer processes.
Keep provider-specific Git-hosting mechanics inside the adapter and branch-update, required-gate, carry-forward, and merge-authorization policy inside the orchestrator.
Publish versioned protocol/price/profile/event mappings; preflight required skills/capabilities before charging; normalize the Appendix B event/error contract; and keep orchestration provider-blind.
Agent adapter must launch in the workspace with scrubbed environment, speak its protocol, and preflight compatibility before charging.
Stream normalized events through bounded backpressure or fail-fast overflow.
Enforce read, turn, and token limits and one role-matched schema-constrained terminal-result channel.
Do not change model or profile within an Attempt or thread, including salvage.
Terminate the full process tree and confirm exit on every end state.
Agent tools must not execute tracker or repository mutations directly; they may only request actions in terminal results.
Parse one repo-owned `WORKFLOW.md` as YAML front matter plus a strict Markdown prompt template exposing the named variables and maintaining the synthesized-rules marker block.
UI changes must not rewrite or commit `WORKFLOW.md`; they create durable overrides.
Apply trusted workflow-path precedence and the specified front-matter/body parsing rules; fail startup for missing files, malformed front matter, empty prompt bodies, and template parse errors.
Detect workflow-file changes; keep last known good state and surface invalid reload errors in logs, events, and UI.
Apply `hot`, `attempt`, `restart`, `bootstrap`, `ack+`, and `file-ack+` exactly as defined, including no partial restart application and source-sensitive acknowledgment.

Accept bootstrap values only from trusted process arguments or host environment; reject them in workflow front matter and overrides, and show only redacted effective values and trusted sources read-only.
`workflow.path` has type path, default `<process-cwd>/WORKFLOW.md`, and reload policy `bootstrap`.
`tracker.kind` has type string, default (required), and reload policy `ack+restart`.
`tracker.owner` has type string, default (required for github), and reload policy `ack+restart`.
`tracker.project_number` has type integer, default (required for github), and reload policy `ack+restart`.
`tracker.repo_owner` has type string, default (required for github), and reload policy `ack+restart`.
`tracker.repo_name` has type string, default (required for github), and reload policy `ack+restart`.
`tracker.status_field` has type string, default `"Status"`, and reload policy `ack+hot`.
`tracker.priority_field` has type string, default `"Priority"`, and reload policy `hot`.
`tracker.priority_order` has type list of strings, default `["P0", "Urgent", "Critical", "P1", "High", "P2", "Medium", "P3", "Low"]`, and reload policy `hot`.
`tracker.acceptance_criteria_heading` has type string, default `"Acceptance Criteria"`, and reload policy `ack+hot`.
`tracker.assignee` has type string or null, default `null`, and reload policy `ack+hot`.
`tracker.required_labels` has type list of strings, default `[]`, and reload policy `ack+hot`.
`polling.interval_ms` has type integer, default `30000`, and reload policy `hot`.
`workspace.root` has type path, default `<system-temp>/symphony_workspaces`, and reload policy `ack+restart`.
`workspace.verify_command` has type string, default (required; literal `none` permitted), and reload policy `ack+attempt`.
`workspace.verify_none_reason` has type string, default `null` (required if `none`), and reload policy `attempt`.
`hooks.after_create` has type script, default `null`, and reload policy `ack+attempt`.
`hooks.before_run` has type script, default `null`, and reload policy `ack+attempt`.
`hooks.after_run` has type script, default `null`, and reload policy `ack+attempt`.
`hooks.before_remove` has type script, default `null`, and reload policy `ack+attempt`.
`hooks.timeout_ms` has type integer, default `60000`, and reload policy `attempt`.
`agent.command` has type string, default `"codex app-server"`, and reload policy `ack+attempt`.
`agent.read_timeout_ms` has type integer, default `5000`, and reload policy `attempt`.
`agent.max_concurrent` has type integer, default `4`, and reload policy `file-ack+hot`.
`agent.max_turns` has type integer, default `8`, and reload policy `file-ack+attempt`.
`agent.turn_timeout_ms` has type integer, default `900000`, and reload policy `file-ack+attempt`.
`agent.stall_timeout_ms` has type integer, default `300000` (`<=0` disables), and reload policy `file-ack+hot`.
`agent.max_failure_retries` has type integer, default `2`, and reload policy `file-ack+attempt`.
`agent.max_rework_cycles` has type integer, default `2`, and reload policy `file-ack+attempt`.
`agent.max_plan_revisions` has type integer, default `2`, and reload policy `file-ack+attempt`.
`agent.max_escalations` has type integer, default `1`, and reload policy `file-ack+attempt`.
`agent.max_retry_backoff_ms` has type integer, default `300000`, and reload policy `hot`.
`agent.required_skills` has type list of names, default `[]`, and reload policy `file-ack+attempt`.
`agent.approval_policy` has type adapter-defined value, default (required), and reload policy `ack+attempt`.
`agent.thread_sandbox` has type adapter-defined value, default (required), and reload policy `ack+attempt`.
`agent.turn_sandbox_policy` has type adapter-defined value, default (required), and reload policy `ack+attempt`.
`budget.per_attempt_tokens` has type integer, default `400000`, and reload policy `file-ack+attempt`.
`budget.per_attempt_usd` has type number, default `5.00`, and reload policy `file-ack+attempt`.
`budget.per_issue_usd` has type number, default `10.00`, and reload policy `file-ack+hot`.
`budget.rolling_24h_usd` has type number, default `50.00`, and reload policy `file-ack+hot`.
`budget.per_issue_tokens` has type integer, default `2000000`, and reload policy `file-ack+hot`.
`budget.rolling_24h_tokens` has type integer, default `10000000`, and reload policy `file-ack+hot`.
`budget.estimate_tokens_by_profile` has type map profile → integer, default `{economy: 100000, standard: 200000, deep: 300000}`, and reload policy `file-ack+hot`.
`budget.history_min_samples` has type integer, default `10`, and reload policy `file-ack+hot`.
`budget.history_window_samples` has type integer, default `50`, and reload policy `file-ack+hot`.
`compute.enabled_profiles` has type list, default `[economy, standard, deep]`, and reload policy `file-ack+attempt`.
`compute.route_profiles` has type map role/class → logical profile, default defaults of Section 8, and reload policy `file-ack+attempt`.
`compute.risk_floor_rules` has type ordered rule list, default built-in rules of Section 4, and reload policy `file-ack+attempt`.
`class.trivial_max_changed_lines` has type integer, default `25`, and reload policy `file-ack+attempt`.
`class.trivial_patterns` has type list of globs, default `[]`, and reload policy `file-ack+attempt`.
`class.risk_paths` has type list of globs, default `[]`, and reload policy `file-ack+attempt`.
`review.max_parallel_specialists` has type integer, default `2`, and reload policy `file-ack+attempt`.
`review.specialists` has type list, default defaults of Section 9.1, and reload policy `file-ack+attempt`.
`review.required_checks` has type list of names, default `[]`, and reload policy `file-ack+hot`.
`review.accepted_check_conclusions` has type list, default `[success, neutral, skipped]`, and reload policy `file-ack+hot`.
`review.snapshot_timeout_ms` has type integer, default `30000`, and reload policy `hot`.
`review.settle_timeout_ms` has type integer, default `1800000`, and reload policy `hot`.
`review.quiet_period_ms` has type integer, default `0`, and reload policy `hot`.
`quality.audit_rate` has type number 0–1, default `0.10`, and reload policy `file-ack+hot`.
`quality.escape_window_days` has type integer, default `14`, and reload policy `file-ack+hot`.
`learning.interval_issues` has type integer, default `25`, and reload policy `hot`.
`learning.max_rules` has type integer, default `25`, and reload policy `attempt`.
`learning.max_prompt_tokens` has type integer, default `4000`, and reload policy `attempt`.
`learning.rule_decay_issues` has type integer, default `100`, and reload policy `hot`.
`human.operators` has type list of `{id, auth_subject, capabilities[], tracker_login?}`, default (required, non-empty), and reload policy `ack+hot`.
`human.reminder_hours` has type integer, default `24`, and reload policy `hot`.
`notify.command` has type string, default `null`, and reload policy `ack+hot`.
`notify.webhook_url` has type string, default `null`, and reload policy `ack+hot`.
`env.allowlist` has type list of var names, default `[]`, and reload policy `ack+attempt`.
`persistence.database_path` has type path, default `<service-data-root>/symphony-encore.sqlite3`, and reload policy `bootstrap`.
`persistence.lease_ttl_ms` has type integer, default `120000`, and reload policy `hot`.
`persistence.retention_days` has type integer or null, default `null` (indefinite), and reload policy `ack+hot`.
`server.port` has type integer, default `8080`, and reload policy `restart`.
`server.host` has type string, default `"127.0.0.1"`, and reload policy `ack+restart`.
`server.auth_kind` has type string, default (required), and reload policy `ack+restart`.

`server.session_secret` has type secret reference, default (required unless auth adapter supplies sessions), and reload policy `ack+restart`. The resolved secret value must never be persisted or returned.

`bootstrap.admin_credential` has type secret reference, default (required only while operator store is empty), and reload policy `bootstrap`. The resolved secret value must never be persisted or returned.
`ui.live_refresh_ms` has type integer, default `1000`, and reload policy `hot`.
Require secret-bearing values to use non-empty `$VAR` indirection.
Expand `~` in path values, resolve a relative `workspace.root` from the workflow directory, and normalize the effective root to an absolute path.
Keep extensions below `extensions.<name>`; warn for unknown forward-compatible keys, but reject safety-critical near misses using the named namespaces and distance rule.
Include full and closest-known keys in configuration warnings/errors and reject blank strings in list values.
Fail startup on startup-validation failure; on per-tick failure skip dispatch but continue reconciliation; on invalid reload preserve last known good config and emit an operator-visible error.

Validate the complete minimum matrix for tracker schema, verification, commands, skills, adapter posture, positive limits/budgets/estimates, history windows, profiles/routes/check conclusions, operators/capabilities, authentication, and local writable compatible database.
In bootstrap, exempt only the empty-store operator/auth-subject mapping; still validate the auth adapter and every other field, and require full validation before orchestration.
When configured, require a materially unchanged complete PR snapshot for the quiet period; timeout pending evidence to Human(blocked), not rework.
Resolve effective configuration in this order: built-in defaults, workflow front matter, then active durable overrides.
Store every non-bootstrap override with key, typed value or reference, version, creator/time/reason, validation, acknowledgment, reload state, and optional expiry.
Require expected version and idempotency key for override create/update/clear; clearing exposes the current workflow/default candidate and preserves any acknowledgment or restart pending state.

Show workflow and effective values, source, candidate version, and pending state; do not let workflow reload overwrite an active override; reject invalid overrides with `422` while preserving last known good config.
Apply overrides only after durable write, validation, acknowledgment, and reload boundary; pin the resulting immutable snapshot to every new Attempt and SystemJob.
Do not accept or return resolved secret values in the UI; operators may set only `$VAR` or adapter-owned references resolved at the trusted boundary.

Core Conformance requires every MUST in §§1–18 plus transactional SQLite, one conforming tracker/repository/agent adapter, authenticated API/UI, indefinite history, orchestration/recovery, budgets, review, merge, and deterministic matrix tests.
Any advertised extension must publish schema, authority, failure, durability, UI, and test contracts.
Every extension must preserve Core invariants.
Pass the Real Integration Profile before declaring production readiness.
A conformance report names the implementation/spec versions, adapters and versions, enabled extensions, test command, deterministic and real-integration results, and implementation-defined choices.
Do not report Core Conformance while any Core requirement is partial.
Ship automated deterministic tests for every Core Conformance matrix item.
Test workflow precedence/parsing/reload and every named malformed, unknown, near-miss, empty, and literal-secret case.
Test precedence, metadata, restart persistence, clear fallback, stale-version rejection, and last-known-good preservation.
Test bootstrap keys are read-only and reject durable overrides.
Test bootstrap keys are rejected in `WORKFLOW.md` and accepted only from trusted startup inputs.
Test ack/file-ack candidate holding, exact-hash acknowledgment, authorized override boundary, and UI/API pending state.

Test pristine exact-hash bootstrap and non-pristine corruption recovery, including disabled mutation/dispatch, rollback, credential rejection, process termination, intent query, and fail-closed restore.
Test required-skill and adapter-capability preflight before charging an Attempt.
Test atomic claim/Attempt/reservation acquisition, duplicate exclusion, receipt-gated issue stage opening, and transition nullability.
Test lease renewal, durable non-expiring claims, and full restart reconstruction of actions, timers, parking, questions, approvals, cursors, stages, and rolling budgets.
Test process ownership verification/termination, interrupted closure, and no blind resume.
Test idempotent unresolved-intent reconciliation and the full external-adapter authorization mismatch matrix.
Test dispatch/mutation shutdown on persistence failure and worker stop when accounting/commit becomes unsafe.
Test malformed/duplicate results, completed evidence, independent verification, failed rework, and atomic role-result/review persistence.
Test Plan criterion/command validation, provisional standard-to-trivial, monotonic class, and newly required gates.
Test all change-class routes, PlanReview decisions/caps/readiness, rework/no-progress, factual handoff, and conditional fields for every role result.
Test one pinned profile/model per Attempt/thread and separately accounted salvage.
Test replay-safe absolute tokens and durable UsageSamples/totals across every listed aggregation.
Test pre-dispatch/fan-out reservation, no-fit denial, actual replacement, atomic top-up, stop-before-turn, release, and overrun blocking.
Test priced USD and unpriced token scopes, hard caps, authored handoff, park/pause/resume/reset, audit, and immutable history.
Test total elapsed and per-stage duration reconstruction across restart.
Test current-head checks, accepted conclusions, threads, blocking reviews, and authorized supervised approval.
Test immutable complete ReviewSets, no comment evidence, no voting, narrow adjudication, and retained uncontested blockers.
Test serialized base-update handling, patch identity, linked carry-forward records, current-head evidence, and changed-patch fresh review.
Test linked repair PRs, independent queues, escaped-defect records, demotion, notification, and durable evidence.
Test sampled audits, lessons/repair, budgeted synthesis, saturation, and supervised merge.
Test operations, history, and settings/control UI using only Control API data.
Test complete stage/log/action/attempt/run/evidence/usage/settings/audit/link history after completion and restart.
Test durable scrubbed normalized action logs grouped by stage with bounded references across restart.
Test UI settings mutations, committed-only display, and preserved submitted/error state for all failure classes.
Test auth, capabilities, CSRF, versions, idempotency, structured errors, redaction, approval scope, full audit, and correct internal-versus-external mutation records.
Test hostile UI content, unsafe links, and CSP without breaking history inspection.
Test indefinite default retention and audited finite deletion with protected records and aggregate tombstones.
Test write-boundary attacks, ownership, quarantine, and full process-tree termination.
Test allowlisted isolated worker environments and absence of tracker/repository/control credentials for agents/reviewers.
Test all external mutations through typed authorized intent/receipt adapters and all control mutations through authenticated versioned API/OperatorAction.

Run a real Todo→In Progress→Review→Done smoke covering plan/class/workspace/agent/verify/PR/checks/approval/merge/post-merge/UI/log/stage/usage/restart, plus question, stale mutation, acknowledgment, budget denial, idempotent replay, and repair PR; retain identifiers and redacted evidence.

Preserve the reference start, dispatch, finish-attempt, override, and control-mutation invariants: single-writer/migrations, pristine bootstrap, recovery-before-ready, atomic claim/budget/intent/result/state, receipt reconciliation, version/idempotency audit, last-known-good config, and external-work-only authorization/intents.
Normalize every listed upstream event with common identity/process fields and every listed error code/failure-class mapping, including hard-cap authored failures and malformed/progress semantics.
Emit detectable startup protocol/capability/schema failures before charging the execution.
Return an unsupported-tool failure to the session and continue instead of stalling.
Orchestrator decisions must depend only on normalized events, never provider payloads or humanized strings.

Codex profile must use bounded stdio, generated schema compatibility, pre-charge negotiation, workspace cwd/posture, role tools/title, prompt/continuation behavior, absolute tokens, approval policy, and confirmed process-group shutdown.
